title: Outcome-Driven Innovation Matrix — Elevator Guide Rails (NAICS 333921)

sections.title: ODI Methodology
sections.content:
Using Ulwick's Outcome-Driven Innovation framework, each desired outcome is scored on Importance (1-10) and Satisfaction (1-10). The **Opportunity Algorithm** identifies underserved and overserved outcomes: [SRC: Ulwick ODI]

> **Opportunity Score = Importance + max(Importance - Satisfaction, 0)**

- Score >= 12: **Highly underserved** (strong innovation opportunity)
- Score 10-12: **Underserved** (meaningful opportunity)
- Score < 10 and Satisfaction > Importance: **Overserved** (cost reduction opportunity)

sections.title: Full Outcome Matrix
sections.content:
| # | Desired Outcome | Job Step | Importance | Satisfaction | Opp Score | Status |
|---|----------------|----------|-----------|-------------|-----------|--------|
| O1 | Minimize deviation between rail profile and ISO 7465 nominal dimensions | Define req. | 9 | 6 | 12 | UNDERSERVED |
| O2 | Minimize time to identify qualified suppliers for a new rail profile size | Locate suppliers | 7 | 5 | 9 | APPROPRIATELY SERVED |
| O3 | Minimize variation between sample rails and production batch rails | Evaluate samples | 9 | 5 | 13 | HIGHLY UNDERSERVED |
| O4 | Minimize rail head straightness deviation from 0.3mm/m tolerance | Evaluate samples | 10 | 6 | 14 | HIGHLY UNDERSERVED |
| O5 | Minimize total cost of ownership per meter of installed guide rail | Negotiate terms | 8 | 6 | 10 | UNDERSERVED |
| O6 | Minimize lead time from purchase order to delivery (repeat orders) | Order & schedule | 8 | 5 | 11 | UNDERSERVED |
| O7 | Minimize lead time for first order of a new profile size | Order & schedule | 7 | 4 | 10 | UNDERSERVED |
| O8 | Minimize probability of receiving out-of-spec rails | Receive & inspect | 9 | 6 | 12 | UNDERSERVED |
| O9 | Minimize effort required for incoming quality inspection | Receive & inspect | 8 | 5 | 11 | UNDERSERVED |
| O10 | Minimize time to align consecutive rail sections in the hoistway | Install | 10 | 7 | 13 | HIGHLY UNDERSERVED |
| O11 | Minimize number of rail sections requiring on-site rework | Install | 9 | 7 | 11 | UNDERSERVED |
| O12 | Minimize ride quality deviation during commissioning test | Validate | 10 | 8 | 12 | UNDERSERVED |
| O13 | Minimize risk of TUeV / notified body rejection at final inspection | Validate | 10 | 8 | 12 | UNDERSERVED |
| O14 | Minimize CO2 footprint per meter of guide rail | Emerging | 5 | 3 | 7 | APPROPRIATELY SERVED |
| O15 | Minimize packaging waste from guide rail deliveries | Emerging | 4 | 5 | 4 | OVERSERVED |

sections.title: Opportunity Landscape
sections.content:
| Category | Count | Outcomes | Strategic Implication |
|----------|-------|----------|----------------------|
| Highly underserved (>= 13) | 3 | O3, O4, O10 | Primary innovation targets — ZOLLERN should lead here |
| Underserved (10-12) | 8 | O1, O5, O6, O7, O8, O9, O11, O12, O13 | Secondary targets — improve vs. incumbents |
| Appropriately served (7-9) | 2 | O2, O14 | Maintain parity |
| Overserved (<7) | 1 | O15 | Cost reduction possible |

sections.title: ZOLLERN Capability vs. Top Opportunities
sections.content:
| Outcome | Opp Score | ZOLLERN Capability | Can Address? | How? |
|---------|-----------|-------------------|-------------|------|
| O3 (Sample-production consistency) | 13 | Cpk > 1.33 on cold-drawn profiles | YES | Statistical process control, same tooling for sample and production |
| O4 (Straightness) | 14 | Roller straightening to 0.5mm/m | PARTIAL | Invest in precision straightening (target 0.2mm/m) |
| O10 (Alignment time) | 13 | Joint face machining +/-0.05mm | YES | Tighter joint face tolerance = faster alignment in shaft |
| O6 (Repeat lead time) | 11 | 4-6 weeks with existing tooling | YES | Dedicated tooling stock, pull-based scheduling |
| O8 (Out-of-spec risk) | 12 | In-line 100% inspection possible | YES | Automated dimensional gauging after straightening |
| O12 (Ride quality) | 12 | Hardened running surface + straightness | PARTIAL | Combined improvement in O4 + hardness uniformity |

sections.title: Innovation Priority Map
sections.content:
| Priority | Outcome | Investment Required | Impact on Market Entry |
|----------|---------|-------------------|----------------------|
| P1 | O4 — Rail head straightness | EUR 200-400K (precision straightening line) | HIGH — differentiator vs. all incumbents |
| P2 | O3 — Sample-production consistency | EUR 50K (SPC system + capability study) | HIGH — builds trust with new OEM customers |
| P3 | O10 — Alignment time reduction | EUR 15-20K (joint face machining tooling) | HIGH — directly reduces customer installation cost |
| P4 | O8 — Zero defect delivery | EUR 100-150K (in-line automated inspection) | MEDIUM — reduces incoming inspection at customer |
| P5 | O6 — Repeat lead time | EUR 0 (operational — dedicated stock) | MEDIUM — improves customer experience |
